channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rubio declares South African ambassador to US 'persona non grata' over Trump comments
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio declared South African ambassador to the U.S. Ebrahim Rasool “persona non grata” and accused the diplomat of being a “race-baiting politician” over his recent comments about President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Thousands converging on Serbia's capital for a major rally that tests Vucic's populist government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people swarmed the streets in Serbia's capital Belgrade on Friday, staging a joyful prelude to a major anti-government rally on the weekend and dealing a blow to populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mark Carney is sworn in as Canada's new prime minister as country deals with Trump's trade war
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former central banker Mark Carney was sworn in as Canada’s new prime minister on Friday, and will now try to steer his country through a trade war brought by U.S. President Donald Trump, annexation threats and an expected federal election.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: State Department imposes sanctions on Thai officials after Uyghur men are deported to China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department on Friday announced sanctions on an unknown number of current and former Thai officials for their role in deporting at least 40 Uyghur men to China despite worries that they could be jailed or even face death there.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Most Americans disapprove of President Trump’s approach to the country's relationship with Russia and his handling of the situation in Ukraine, according to a new survey published on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: US envoy is taking Putin's comments on Ukraine ceasefire proposal to Trump, Kremlin official says
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff to discuss details of the American proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Ukraine, asking him to convey Moscow's thoughts to Washington, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: West Virginia Republican's bill would ban visas for Chinese students
item.description: Freshman Rep. Riley Moore is backing legislation that would prohibit all Chinese nationals from obtaining student visas, in an effort to prevent them from spying on the American government or stealing advanced technology.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: House GOP promotes use of 'Taiwan' over 'Chinese Taipei' in slight to Beijing
item.description: A group of House Republicans is introducing legislation to assert Taiwan as the official name of the independently-governed island in U.S. communication, pushing back against claims to the territory by the Chinese government in Beijing. Rep. Byron Donalds (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Waltz: Russia, Ukraine peace deal likely to include Donbas region cession
item.description: National security adviser Mike Waltz said Ukraine likely would have to cede some of its Donbas region to Russia if it wants a long-term peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Killing site in Jalisco, Mexico just the latest in a long series of gruesome discoveries
item.description: Prosecutors in the western Mexican state of Jalisco have confirmed the discovery of many clothing items and bone fragments by a group of people searching for relatives at a previously known cartel training site.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel faces new Syria challenge as it adjusts to new strategy amid regional power struggle for influence
item.description: Israel is rethinking its Syria strategy following the country's Islamist takeover in December, rising attacks on minorities and growing regional power struggles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Irish golf course damaged by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: The incident comes less than a week after pro-Palestinian protestors dug up some of the Trump Turnberry greens in Scotland.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Mark Carney is sworn in as Canada's new prime minister
item.description: Carney, a former Goldman Sachs executive with no experience in politics, becomes Canada's 24th prime minister.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN report warns Iran is stepping up electronic surveillance of women to enforce headscarf laws
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Iran is increasingly relying on electronic surveillance and the public to inform on women refusing to wear the country's mandatory headscarf in public, as hard-liners push for harsher penalties for those protesting the law, a United Nations report released Friday found.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Renowned Mexico City restaurant serves traditional street food and nostalgia of the homeland
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Quintonil is not your typical Mexican restaurant. Clients book tables months in advance to celebrate special occasions. The World’s 50 Best list ranked it as the most acclaimed venue in the country in 2024 — and No. 7 worldwide.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy accuses Putin of trying to stall ceasefire talks, pushes Trump for tougher sanctions
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is pushing President Donald Trump to impose sanctions on Russia, as he believes Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to prolong the war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas says it will release a US-Israeli hostage and 4 bodies, but Israel expresses immediate doubt
item.description: Hamas says it has accepted a proposal from mediators to release one living American-Israeli hostage and the bodies of four dual nationals who had died in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippine ex-President Duterte appears by video link at International Criminal Court hearing
item.description: Jailed former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte has appeared by videoconference before judges at the International Criminal Court.
item.dc:creator: Mike Corder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Millions of people celebrate Holi, the Hindu festival of colors
item.description: Millions of people in South Asia are celebrating Holi, the Hindu festival of colors, by smearing each other with brightly colored powder, dancing to festive music and feasting on traditional sweets prepared for the occasion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ontario Premier Ford hails 'positive' meeting with Lutnick
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford lauded his Thursday meeting with Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick as “positive” and “productive” after their public rift over tariffs on imported goods.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Syrian leader signs constitution that puts the country under an Islamist group's rule for 5 years
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria's interim president on Thursday signed a temporary constitution that leaves the country under Islamist rule while promising to protect the rights of all Syrians for five years during a transitional phase.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll: Majority of Americans think Trump is too close to Russia
item.description: A majority of Americans think President Trump is too closely aligned with Russia, including more than a quarter of Republicans, a poll released Thursday found.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Sales of US goods 'rapidly dropping' at Canadian grocery stores
item.description: Canadians are buying fewer products made in the U.S. in an effort to brace for the impact of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs, which are set to cause prices to skyrocket across a range of products.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: UN-backed experts accuse Israel of sexual and gender-based violence in Gaza
item.description: A United Nations-backed group of human rights experts accused Israel of “disproportionate violence against women and children” during the war against Hamas in Gaza, and accused Israeli security forces of rape and sexual violence against Palestinian detainees.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'hopes' Putin agrees to ceasefire as Moscow signals no truce yet
item.description: President Donald Trump said he "hopes" Russia agrees to a ceasefire deal after Ukraine accepted the terms earlier this week and warned it would be a "very disappointing moment for the world" otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pushes annexation of Greenland during meeting with NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday expressed confidence the United States would annex Greenland, even suggesting the head of the NATO alliance could be a key player in facilitating the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Peru's detained ex-President Castillo transferred to a hospital days after starting hunger strike
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Former Peruvian President Pedro Castillo was taken from prison to a hospital on Thursday, three days after he began a hunger strike in protest of his ongoing rebellion trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says he agrees in principle with US proposal for 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine
item.description: “So the idea itself is correct, and we certainly support it,” Putin told a news conference in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens 200% tariff on European wine after proposed whiskey tariff
item.description: Trump vowed a new escalation in his trade war if the EU pushes ahead with the planned 50% tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Search for Sudiksha Konanki, missing Pitt student, continues in Dominican Republic
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki was last seen early Thursday morning on a beach near Punta Cana. Police have spoken with a man allegedly with her at the time.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Pope gets good news from doctors: He's no longer in immediate danger
item.description: Doctors are still cautious and have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Palestinian activists vandalize Trump golf course in Scotland
item.description: In white bold-face capital letters, activists wrote “Gaza is not 4 sale” on Trump’s Turnberry golf course.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Panama releases dozens of detained deportees from US into limbo following human rights criticism
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — After weeks of lawsuits and human rights criticism, Panama on Saturday released dozens of migrants who were held for weeks in a remote camp after being deported from the United States, telling them they have 30 days to leave the Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: BY MEGAN JANETSKY, ALMA SOLÍS and MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation
